perturbation.original_text: BOSCH reserves the right to share rights given unless it disrupts and/or interferes with CLIENTS business and/or productivity.
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH reserves the right to share rights given regardless of whether it disrupts and/or interferes with CLIENTS business and/or productivity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, BOSCH could only share rights if it didn't disrupt the CLIENT's business. Now, BOSCH can share rights even if it does disrupt the CLIENT, contradicting the implicit understanding of mutual benefit within the strategic alliance. This directly contradicts the 'Reserved Rights' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that BOSCH can share rights 'regardless of whether it disrupts and/or interferes with CLIENT'S business', which directly contradicts the 'Reserved Rights' section. The original text limited BOSCH's right to share rights only if it didn't disrupt the CLIENT's business and/or productivity. The new text removes this limitation, creating a situation where BOSCH can now act in a way that negatively impacts the client, directly conflicting with the spirit and implied protection within the 'RESERVED RIGHTS' section which is meant to be in concert and approval with BOSCH and limited to BOSCH's current clients.

perturbation.original_text: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH hereby revokes CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction. The original text grants the CLIENT un-exclusive rights. The modified text revokes those same rights, leading to uncertainty about whether the CLIENT can sell and distribute the product within the territory under reserved rights. This contradicts the earlier grant of rights in Section 5. RIGHTS GRANTED: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory".
perturbation.contradicted_text: RIGHTS GRANTED: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby revokes CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.justification: The original text granted the client 'un-exclusive' reserved rights, while the changed text revokes those rights. This directly contradicts the 'RIGHTS GRANTED' section, which states BOSCH grants CLIENT 'Exclusive Distribution License Rights', creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding who bears the shipping costs. The original text assigns this responsibility to the CLIENT, while the modified text assigns it to BOSCH. This directly contradicts Section 8(iv) which states: (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. BOSCH is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery costs, while the changed text assigns this responsibility to BOSCH. The contradicted text in Section 8(iv) clearly states that 'All Costs...as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT...is the sole responsibility of CLIENT,' directly contradicting the modified text that places this burden on BOSCH. The contradicted text goes on to also say 'All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. BOSCH is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.' which would contradict the original statement that CLIENT is responsible for those same costs. This inconsistency creates a direct and impactful conflict within the document regarding financial obligations.
